passage: When Community Junior College introduced a school garden, many students first thought it was only another way of making them work. The principal, however, explained that the garden was meant to serve as a practical learning centre. Science teachers used it to teach plant growth, while Home Economics teachers showed students how vegetables could be prepared into healthy meals. At first, only a few students showed interest. Later, as the crops began to grow, more students became curious and started taking part in watering, weeding and record keeping. Parents who visited the school praised the project, and some even donated seeds and tools. By the end of the term, the garden had become both a source of food and a source of pride for the school.
question: The main idea of the passage is that the school garden

options.A: was introduced mainly to punish lazy students
options.B: became useful as both a learning centre and a source of pride
options.C: was disliked by all the teachers in the school
options.D: produced enough food for the whole community

answer_text: became useful as both a learning centre and a source of pride

question: The writer's purpose in the passage is to

options.A: show how a simple school project can become educationally valuable
options.B: argue that students should spend all their time farming
options.C: prove that only parents can improve schools
options.D: criticize teachers for giving practical lessons

answer_text: show how a simple school project can become educationally valuable

options.A: were forced to work without explanation
options.B: saw practical results from the project

passage: Bisi had always enjoyed cycling to school, especially because it saved her the cost of transport. One Monday morning, however, she noticed that the road was unusually busy. Heavy vehicles moved noisily, impatient traders pushed handcarts across the street, and some school children ran carelessly between moving cars. Bisi remembered the road safety lesson her class teacher had given the previous week. Instead of trying to ride quickly through the confusion, she got off her bicycle at the most dangerous points and walked it across. Although this delayed her a little, she arrived safely and still in good time for assembly. Later that day, the teacher used Bisi's decision as an example of how caution can prevent accidents.
question: The central idea of the passage is that

options.A: cycling is always faster than public transport
options.B: busy roads should be closed to students
options.C: careful behaviour on the road can prevent accidents
options.D: teachers should accompany all students to school

answer_text: careful behaviour on the road can prevent accidents

options.A: Her bicycle had developed a fault

options.D: She was already late for assembly
